 {"id":"frankenredis-e3hr","title":"Implement LATENCY HISTOGRAM per-command tracking","description":"## What
LATENCY HISTOGRAM currently returns an empty array (stub). Redis returns cumulative distribution
data for per-command latencies when enabled.

## Location
crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12152-12158

## Current behavior
```rust
// LATENCY HISTOGRAM [command ...]
// Returns cumulative distribution of latencies for commands.
// Currently returns an empty array as per-command latency histograms
// are not yet tracked. Full implementation would return map-like data.
// TODO: Implement full histogram tracking per-command.
Ok(RespFrame::Array(Some(vec![])))
```

## Design
1. Add HdrHistogram or similar per-command latency tracking to Store or Runtime
2. Record command execution times in dispatch_argv return path
3. Return histogram bucket data in Redis-compatible format

## Acceptance criteria
- LATENCY HISTOGRAM returns actual per-command latency distributions
- Compatible with Redis LATENCY HISTOGRAM output format
- Minimal overhead when not actively querying histograms","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:03:01.317581137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T00:15:49.894705874Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T00:15:49.891747186Z","close_reason":"Implemented LATENCY HISTOGRAM per-command tracking with power-of-2 microsecond buckets, integrated latency recording at all command dispatch points","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-egag","title":"Track real XINFO STREAM radix-tree and deletion metadata","description":"# Track real XINFO STREAM metrics

## What
XINFO STREAM currently returns placeholder values for:
- radix-tree-keys: hardcoded to 1 (should be count of distinct stream entry IDs)
- radix-tree-nodes: hardcoded to 2 (should reflect internal tree structure)
- max-deleted-entry-id: hardcoded to "0-0" (should track highest deleted ID)

## Design
Redis internally uses a radix tree for stream entries. FrankenRedis uses a BTreeMap, so:
- radix-tree-keys: report the number of entries (same as stream length, since each entry is a unique key)
- radix-tree-nodes: report entries + 1 (approximate; radix tree always has at least one node more than keys)
- max-deleted-entry-id: add a new field to Store's stream metadata tracking the highest ID ever deleted via XDEL or XTRIM

## Implementation
1. Add pub stream_max_deleted_ids: HashMap<Vec<u8>, StreamId> to Store
2. In Store::xdel(): update max_deleted_id for the stream key if the deleted ID is higher
3. In Store::xtrim(): same logic for trimmed entries
4. In XINFO STREAM handler: read stream length for radix-tree-keys, length+1 for radix-tree-nodes, max_deleted_id for max-deleted-entry-id

## Testing
- Unit test: XADD entries, XDEL some, verify XINFO STREAM reports correct max-deleted-entry-id
- Conformance: add XINFO STREAM cases with deletion

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (stream_max_deleted_ids field, xdel/xtrim methods)
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (XINFO STREAM handler ~line 5132)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:30.104773064Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T18:52:08.730212217Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T18:52:08.729950942Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
